verses.text: Woe to Ariel, the city of David's dwelling; God will distress her till her voice is low 'out of the dust.' Jerusalem is humbled. Even the favoured city must feel the rod when she sins.
Woe to Ariel, the city of David's dwelling; God will distress her till her voice is low 'out of the dust.' Jerusalem is humbled. Even the favoured city must feel the rod when she sins.
Woe to Ariel, the city of David's dwelling; God will distress her till her voice is low 'out of the dust.' Jerusalem is humbled. Even the favoured city must feel the rod when she sins.
Woe to Ariel, the city of David's dwelling; God will distress her till her voice is low 'out of the dust.' Jerusalem is humbled. Even the favoured city must feel the rod when she sins.

Yet her many foes shall vanish 'as small dust' and as a dream of the night; the nations that fight Zion shall come to nothing. Their triumph is illusory. God can scatter the besieging hosts in a moment.

Yet her many foes shall vanish 'as small dust' and as a dream of the night; the nations that fight Zion shall come to nothing. Their triumph is illusory. God can scatter the besieging hosts in a moment.

Yet her many foes shall vanish 'as small dust' and as a dream of the night; the nations that fight Zion shall come to nothing. Their triumph is illusory. God can scatter the besieging hosts in a moment.

Yet her many foes shall vanish 'as small dust' and as a dream of the night; the nations that fight Zion shall come to nothing. Their triumph is illusory. God can scatter the besieging hosts in a moment.
The LORD pours on them 'a spirit of deep sleep,' closing the eyes of the seers. Spiritual blindness is judgment. Those who will not see are at last unable to see.
The LORD pours on them 'a spirit of deep sleep,' closing the eyes of the seers. Spiritual blindness is judgment. Those who will not see are at last unable to see.
The vision becomes 'a book that is sealed,' which neither learned nor unlearned can read. Truth is hidden from them. When men refuse God's word, its meaning is closed to their hearts.
The vision becomes 'a book that is sealed,' which neither learned nor unlearned can read. Truth is hidden from them. When men refuse God's word, its meaning is closed to their hearts.

'This people draw near me with their mouth... but have removed their heart far from me'; so God will do a marvellous work that confounds the wise. Lip-worship is rejected. God seeks the heart, not mere outward forms.

'This people draw near me with their mouth... but have removed their heart far from me'; so God will do a marvellous work that confounds the wise. Lip-worship is rejected. God seeks the heart, not mere outward forms.
Woe to those who hide their counsel from the LORD; shall the clay say to the potter, 'He made me not'? Such pride is absurd. The creature cannot escape the eye or the hand of his Maker.
Woe to those who hide their counsel from the LORD; shall the clay say to the potter, 'He made me not'? Such pride is absurd. The creature cannot escape the eye or the hand of his Maker.

Soon Lebanon shall be a fruitful field, the deaf shall hear the words of the book, and the meek shall increase their joy. Grace reverses the curse. God opens blind eyes and gives the lowly fresh gladness.

Soon Lebanon shall be a fruitful field, the deaf shall hear the words of the book, and the meek shall increase their joy. Grace reverses the curse. God opens blind eyes and gives the lowly fresh gladness.

Soon Lebanon shall be a fruitful field, the deaf shall hear the words of the book, and the meek shall increase their joy. Grace reverses the curse. God opens blind eyes and gives the lowly fresh gladness.
The terrible and the scorner shall be brought to nought, and those who twist justice cut off. Oppressors meet their end. God removes the wicked who trapped the innocent with a word.
The terrible and the scorner shall be brought to nought, and those who twist justice cut off. Oppressors meet their end. God removes the wicked who trapped the innocent with a word.
Jacob shall no more be ashamed; those who erred in spirit shall come to understanding and learn doctrine. Restoration follows. God turns the wandering heart back to truth and to Himself.
